other_observers: Bill and Jim Schmoker, Brandon Percival; Not sure who first found this bird
specific_location: just a few hundred feet west of the western-most tires of South Shore Marina, Lake Pueblo (Pueblo Reservoir) State Park
description: This was a small gull, slightly smaller than the nearby Ring-billed Gulls. This bird had a medium gray mantle a few shades darker than Ringers, thin unmarked dull yellow bill, rounded head, dark brown eye, large white tertial crescents, and a overall petit look. The head was heavily streaked with brown. Feet were not visible.Behaviors: Loafing in open waters of Pueblo Reservoir.Call: non heardPlumage: adult basic
